title: Apple is bringing age verification to Texas this week
summary: Apple is implementing age verification in the Texas App Store starting June 4th, requiring new account holders to verify they are over 18 using credit cards or government ID. The move follows a federal appeals court decision allowing Texas's App Store Accountability Act to proceed.
content:
Apple will introduce age verification in the App Store for users in Texas starting on Thursday, June 4th. The move, as spotted by MacRumors, comes just days after a federal appeals court allowed Texas' App Store Accountability Act to go into effect while a lawsuit against it proceeds.
People in Texas who are creating a new Apple account will need to verify they're over 18 using a credit card or government ID. Apple may also automatically verify users' age using the age of their account and whether they have a credit card on file.
Meanwhile, users under 18 must join a Family Sharing group, where a parent or guardian will need to provide con …
Read the full story at The Verge.

title: WiiM expands its whole-home ecosystem with a new soundbar
summary: Audio company WiiM is launching the $479 WiiM Bar soundbar in July with a 3.0.2 Atmos configuration featuring eight drivers and expandability, continuing its strategy of offering audiophile-quality sound at accessible prices. The soundbar includes a 2.1-inch touchscreen and integrates with WiiM's smart home ecosystem.
content:
The $479 WiiM Bar includes a 2.1-inch touchscreen on its front. | Image: WiiM	

WiiM, the audio company that's challenged the idea that audiophile-level performance requires a small loan, is expanding its whole-home ecosystem with the WiiM Bar, which releases in July. Much like its other speakers and audio components, the WiiM Bar supports a bunch of streaming options and expandability at an affordable price - in this case, $479.
The company's first soundbar has a 3.0.2 Atmos configuration with a total of eight drivers - three front mid-woofers, three front tweeters, and two up-firing full-range height drivers - paired with four passive radiators. That configuration can be expanded to 3.1.2 with the addition of a WiiM  …
Read the full story at The Verge.

A wave of new startups is chasing those government contracts, but according to Ross Fubini, the venture investor who wrote Anduril’s first check, most of them will get lost in the Valley of Death between prototype contract […]

title: Nvidia is already planning N2X and N3X chips — the goal is the Star Trek computer

Huang indicated collaboration with Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella has been underway for three years.
content:
Jensen Huang, chief executive officer of Nvidia Corp. | Bloomberg via Getty Images	

Just in case you were wondering, Nvidia's RTX Spark isn't supposed to be a one-off. The company is not just flirting with becoming the fifth high-profile vendor of consumer laptop chips to see if people bite. At Computex 2026 in Taipei, Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang confirmed at least two additional generations of RTX Spark are already planned. The eventual goal, he said, is to build Star Trek-like computers and and Star Wars-like droids you can order around with your voice.
"I want to talk to my laptop! I want R2-D2!" he told analysts and investors at Computex, revealing that he started working with Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella "about three years …
Read the full story at The Verge.

title: The best Bluetooth trackers for Apple and Android phones
summary: A guide to Bluetooth trackers for locating lost items, with modern models offering ultra-wideband precision tracking, integration with Find My and Find Hub networks, and improved anti-stalking features.
content:
Some people rarely lose things. Wallets are always exactly where they’re supposed to be, keys never go missing, and remotes never slip between the couch cushions. And then there’s the rest of us — the folks who can’t ever seem to find the thing that was right there a few seconds ago. For us, Bluetooth trackers can help.
Bluetooth trackers have been around for a long time, and they all generally work the same way. You attach the tracker to an object (a key ring, tote, in your wallet, etc), pair it with your phone, and then, if you lose said object, you can go into an app and ring the tracker. These days, Bluetooth trackers can do a lot more. Some have ultra-wideband chips that enable precision tracking, so you can find exactly where in a room your item is without having to sound an alarm. Most trackers tap into large networks — like Apple’s Find My and Google’s Find Hub — making it easier to locate lost items outside the home. Many will notify you if they detect you’ve left the device behind or come with QR codes that link to your contact information so people can easily return lost items.
These features are incredibly handy but also have the potential for misuse. For example, when Apple launched the AirTag, it seems the company hadn’t anticipated they might be used to track people or stolen items — but that’s exactly what happened. It’s since beefed up its anti-stalking features, and companies like Tile have also followed suit. Apple and Google have launched a standard that enables unwanted tracking alerts across both Android and iOS devices, and major players like Tile, Samsung, and Chipolo have supported the initiative. Still, it’s worth making yourself aware of each tracker’s approach to safety when deciding the best option for you.
As consummate Losers of Things, we have tested a fair share of Bluetooth trackers on wallets, keys, and luggage. Here’s what we’d recommend if you, too, have a hard time finding things.





The best tracker for iPhone users
Apple Air

title: The best Qi2 batteries for iPhone and Pixel
summary: A review of the fastest Qi2.2-certified magnetic power banks that support wireless charging up to 25W for iPhone 16 and compatible Android phones, with recommendations for different user needs.
content:
Compact power banks have gotten a lot faster in the past year — and it’s not just their USB-C charging speeds that have received a boost. The newest Qi2.2-certified models can wirelessly charge an iPhone 16 or later at up to 25W. Combine that with their ability to magnetically snap on via MagSafe, and you’ve got yourself an essential add-on that’s easy to take on the go.
Qi2 is a magnetic wireless charging standard that’s based on Apple’s MagSafe tech, so almost all modern iPhones support it. While the latest iPhones offer the fastest charging rates, older models (dating back to the iPhone 12) support 15W Qi2 charging speeds, which is still suitably quick. (The iPhone 17E also maxes out at 15W, and the 16E doesn’t have wireless charging at all.)
On the other hand, Android phones are a mixed bag, with very few models supporting Qi2, let alone Qi2.2. Google’s Pixel 10, 10 Pro, and 10 Pro Fold support Qi2 charging at up to 15W; the 10 Pro XL supports Qi 2.2 at up to 25W. Samsung’s Galaxy 26 lineup is “Qi2 Ready,” which means they can use Qi2 chargers at up to 15W if they have a magnet case. And that’s pretty much it.
We know you want the best, whichever phone you have. Some of you may be willing to pay a lot for it, while others may simply want the best possible model for the least amount of money. Of the seven we tested — which vary in features, design, and charging speed — we landed on two picks that should satisfy most people’s needs. 
The Qi2.2 battery with the fastest wireless charging speeds
Baseus PicoGo AM52 Qi2.2 Magnetic Power Bank

title: As AI gets better, it reveals an empty promise
summary: Google's new Gemini AI agent Spark demonstrates impressive capabilities by accessing personal information like pet names and family details, but critics argue its productivity focus misses pressing real-world problems.
content:
This week we've got tandem hands-ons with Google's new Gemini AI agent - Spark - from my colleagues David Pierce and Jay Peters. Their takeaways are similar: It's so effective that it's scary. Spark knew that David's dog is named Frida and knew the first name of Jay's wife, even though neither of them explicitly provided this information to Google. But what's scary to me is how all of this stuff seems geared toward a future of "productivity" that completely misses what needs to be fixed in our world.
"Productivity" is often pitched as a panacea for what befalls us in our personal lives, even going so far as to implicate our moral worthiness …
Read the full story at The Verge.

title: PlayStation is getting back to what it’s good at
summary: PlayStation announced a strategic refocus on premium, narrative-driven single-player games following costly failures in live-service gaming, showcasing this shift through titles like Marvel's Wolverine.
content:
PlayStation used its most recent State of Play showcase to make it clear where its focus is. After a series of costly live-service stumbles, it's getting back to focusing on premium, narrative-driven, single-player games. That statement was made clear with how it started and ended the hourlong show.
The showcase began with an extended look at gameplay from Marvel's Wolverine, the new superhero title from Insomniac Games. Over seven minutes of bloody action, Logan sliced and diced his way through a bunch of baddies as he tried to rescue some captured mutants, briefly teaming up with Jean Grey for some help taking them down. Insomniac is well …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: PlayStation回归擅长的领域
zh.summary: PlayStation宣布战略重点转向高端、叙事驱动的单人游戏，此前在直播服务游戏领域经历了成本高昂的失败，该转变通过《漫威金刚狼》等游戏来展现。

title: Autonomous vehicles were supposed to cut traffic—what if they don't?
summary: New data reveals that Waymo's robotaxis are empty for nearly half of the miles they operate, raising questions about whether autonomous vehicles will actually reduce traffic as promised. The finding suggests that empty miles driven by robotaxis could potentially contribute to congestion rather than alleviating it. This challenges the prevailing assumption that autonomous vehicle adoption will automatically improve traffic conditions.
content: Data shows Waymo's robotaxis are empty for almost half of the miles they drive.

zh.title: 自动驾驶汽车本应减少交通拥堵——如果它们做不到呢？
zh.summary: 新数据显示Waymo的机器人出租车空驶里程占其运营总里程的近一半，这引发了人们对自动驾驶汽车是否真的能如承诺那样减少交通的质疑。这一发现表明，机器人出租车的空驶里程可能会加剧拥堵，而不是缓解拥堵。这对自动驾驶汽车采用会自动改善交通状况的普遍假设提出了挑战。

title: These two founders left Goldman and Meta to build voice AI for markets everyone else overlooked
summary: A startup founded by ex-Goldman and Meta employees built a voice AI platform for underserved markets in Africa and the Middle East, now handling over 17,000 calls daily.
content: The startup's own stack for Africa and Middle East is now handling more than 17,000 calls per day.

zh.title: 这两位创始人离开高盛和Meta，为被忽视的市场开发语音AI
zh.summary: 由高盛和Meta前员工创办的初创公司为非洲和中东的服务不足的市场构建了语音AI平台，目前每天处理超过17,000通电话。

title: Supernatural isn’t dead after all
summary: Meta announced Supernatural, its popular VR fitness game, is being spun off into an independent company called Supernatural Health, reversing plans to discontinue development after VR layoffs.
content:
DeeDee Henry works out using VR at her home in Ventura, California. | Photo by Maggie Shannon / The Verge	

A few months ago, Meta effectively handed Supernatural, a popular VR fitness game on the Meta Quest, a death sentence. As part of overarching VR layoffs, the company announced the game would no longer get any new content, enraging its tightly knit, devoted community. Now it looks like Supernatural is getting a second chance. Today, Meta announced in a community post that the game is being spun off into an independent company later this year.
The new entity will be called Supernatural Health, and will launch as a separate app on the Meta Horizon Store. While Meta did not comment on who would be the CEO of Supernatural Health, Meta spokespers …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: Supernatural毕竟没有消亡
zh.summary: Meta宣布其受欢迎的VR健身游戏Supernatural将被分拆成一家独立公司Supernatural Health，推翻了在VR裁员后停止开发的计划。

title: GitLab cuts 14% of staff as it scales its platform to serve AI workloads
summary: GitLab is cutting 14% of its workforce while exiting operations in 22 countries and eliminating management layers to focus on scaling its platform for AI workloads. The restructuring reflects the company's strategic prioritization of infrastructure investment and AI capabilities. These changes are intended to streamline operations and position GitLab for growth in emerging AI-driven development tools.
content: The company is reducing its workforce as it exits 22 countries, reduces management layers, and invests in its infrastructure to scale its platform.

zh.title: GitLab裁员14%，扩展其平台以服务AI工作负载
zh.summary: GitLab正在裁员14%，同时退出22个国家的运营，并消除管理层以专注于扩展其平台以处理AI工作负载。重组反映了该公司对基础设施投资和AI能力的战略优先级。这些变化旨在简化运营，并使GitLab为新兴AI驱动开发工具的增长做好准备。

title: Publishers will be able to opt out of AI Search, thanks to new regulation
summary: U.K. regulators have mandated that Google provide a tool enabling website publishers to opt out of having their content featured in generative AI search results. The opt-out feature will first be tested in the United Kingdom before being rolled out globally. This regulatory requirement represents a significant step in giving publishers control over how their content is used in AI systems.
content: U.K. regulators are requiring Google offer a tool allowing website publishers to opt-out of generative AI search features. The option will be tested in the U.K. then rolled out globally.

zh.title: 感谢新监管，出版商将能够选择退出AI搜索
zh.summary: 英国监管机构已强制Google提供一个工具，使网站出版商能够选择退出其内容在生成式AI搜索结果中被展示。该选择退出功能将首先在英国进行测试，然后再全球推出。这一监管要求代表了向出版商提供对其内容在AI系统中如何使用的控制权的重要一步。

title: TikTok launches TikTok Pro Events, an app for cultural moments like the FIFA World Cup
summary: TikTok has launched TikTok Pro Events, a new app designed to enhance engagement during major cultural moments and sporting events like the FIFA World Cup. The app enables users to connect with other fans, discover trending videos, and access curated content from creators. This initiative represents TikTok's strategy to position itself as a central hub for event-driven social engagement.
content: The app allows users to engage with other fans, explore trending videos, and access curated creator feeds.

zh.title: TikTok推出TikTok Pro Events应用，为FIFA世界杯等文化时刻设计
zh.summary: TikTok推出了新应用TikTok Pro Events，旨在增强用户在FIFA世界杯等主要文化和体育赛事期间的参与度。该应用使用户能够与其他粉丝联系、发现热门视频并访问创作者的精选内容。此举代表了TikTok将自己定位为事件驱动型社交参与中心的战略。

title: The worst hacks and breaches of 2026 (so far)
summary: TechCrunch has compiled a list of the most damaging security breaches and hacking incidents of 2026, including a major DOGE data breach and compromises of critical energy, water systems, and an FBI surveillance system. These incidents highlight the expanding scope and severity of cyberattacks targeting both private companies and critical infrastructure. The breaches underscore growing cybersecurity vulnerabilities in key sectors.
content: From a massive DOGE data breach and the hacking of critical energy and water systems to the hack of an FBI surveillance system, here are the most damaging security incidents and data breaches of 2026.

zh.title: 2026年迄今为止最严重的黑客攻击和数据泄露事件
zh.summary: TechCrunch编制了2026年最具破坏性的安全漏洞和黑客攻击事件列表，包括重大DOGE数据泄露以及关键能源、水系统和FBI监视系统的泄露。这些事件突显了针对私营公司和关键基础设施的网络攻击范围和严重程度的扩大。这些泄露事件强调了关键部门日益增长的网络安全漏洞。

title: Meet Wander, a StumbleUpon-inspired tool for discovering the ‘small web’
summary: Wander is an open-source community project that recreates the StumbleUpon experience for discovering lesser-known websites on the "small web." The tool enables users to randomly encounter and recommend favorite sites from across the internet's less-trafficked corners. This initiative reflects a growing interest in decentralized discovery alternatives to algorithm-driven social platforms.
content: This open source community project lets you create a StumbleUpon-like experience for recommending your favorite sites.

zh.title: 认识Wander，一个受StumbleUpon启发的"小网络"发现工具
zh.summary: Wander是一个开源社区项目，重现了StumbleUpon在"小网络"上发现鲜为人知网站的体验。该工具使用户能够随机发现并推荐来自互联网流量较少区域的喜爱网站。此举反映了人们对分散式发现替代品的日益增长的兴趣，以应对算法驱动的社交平台。

title: Meta’s AI agent for WhatsApp Business is now available globally
summary: Meta launched its AI agent for WhatsApp Business globally, with businesses charged on a per-token usage basis for the service.
content: WhatsApp will charge businesses for using its AI agent based on token usage

zh.title: Meta的WhatsApp Business AI助手现已全球推出
zh.summary: Meta在全球推出了WhatsApp Business AI助手，企业将按令牌使用量付费。

title: Inside Meta's attempts to play catch-up with AI
summary: Meta is making significant efforts to compete in the AI space, but questions persist about whether the company can catch up with leading AI competitors like OpenAI and Google. The company faces challenges in developing competitive AI models and infrastructure at the pace of established leaders. Doubts about Meta's ability to close the gap remain despite substantial investment and research initiatives.
content: Doubts linger over whether Meta can close the gap with rivals.

zh.title: Meta 追赶 AI 竞争对手的内幕
zh.summary: Meta 正在做出重大努力在 AI 领域竞争，但人们仍然怀疑该公司是否能追上 OpenAI 和谷歌等领先的 AI 竞争对手。该公司面临着以既有领导者的步伐开发具竞争力的 AI 模型和基础设施的挑战。尽管进行了大量投资和研究，对 Meta 缩小差距能力的疑虑仍然存在。

title: A first look at Microsoft’s Surface Laptop Ultra and Surface Dev Box
summary: Microsoft unveiled two new Surface devices powered by Nvidia's RTX Spark chips—the 15-inch Surface Laptop Ultra and Surface RTX Spark Dev Box—both arriving later this year with 2,000-nit mini LED displays.
content:
The Surface Laptop Ultra.	

Microsoft has two new Surface devices arriving later this year, both powered by Nvidia's RTX Spark chips. I got a chance to take a closer look at both the Surface Laptop Ultra and Surface RTX Spark Dev Box at Microsoft's Build conference this week, and while both have the same chip inside, they're utilizing Nvidia's RTX Spark in different ways.
The Surface Laptop Ultra looks and feels very much like a 16-inch MacBook Pro. There are no transforming hinges, detachable displays, or any other tricks - this is a clamshell laptop built with performance in mind. Microsoft has opted for a 15-inch mini LED panel, which operates at up to 2,000 nits o …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: 微软Surface Laptop Ultra和Surface Dev Box首次亮相
zh.summary: 微软推出两款由英伟达RTX Spark芯片驱动的新Surface设备——15英寸Surface Laptop Ultra和Surface RTX Spark Dev Box，均配备2000尼特迷你LED显示屏，将于今年晚些时候发布。

title: Coralogix raises $200M on bet that someone needs to watch the AI agents
summary: Coralogix raised $200M in Series F funding at a $1.6B valuation, betting on growing demand for observability and monitoring of AI agent systems.
content: The Series F round values Coralogix at $1.6 billion and comes less than a year after its previous raise.

zh.title: Coralogix融资2亿美元，押注AI代理监控市场
zh.summary: Coralogix在F轮融资中筹集2亿美元，估值16亿美元，看好AI代理系统可观测性和监控需求的增长。

title: Plex adds new social features ahead of a major price hike for its lifetime pass
summary: Plex announced new social networking features to compete with Reddit and Letterboxd ahead of a major price increase for its lifetime subscription tier.
content: Plex has come a long way from being just a personal media server. Over the past few years, it has transformed into a streaming hub, today featuring ad-supported content and movie rental options. Now, the company is setting its sights on competing with social networking platforms like Reddit and Letterboxd: on Wednesday, Plex unveiled several […]

zh.title: Plex在提价前推出新社交功能
zh.summary: Plex宣布推出新的社交网络功能，计划在大幅提高终身订阅价格前与Reddit和Letterboxd竞争。

title: SwitchBot’s acquisition of Nanoleaf is about more than lighting
summary: SwitchBot acquired smart lighting company Nanoleaf through parent company OneRobotics, with plans for product integration while keeping Nanoleaf operationally independent.
content:
Nanoleaf has made a name for itself by developing smart color-changing LED lighting products, including wall panels, TV mirroring, and display cases. | Image: Nanoleaf	

Smart lighting company Nanoleaf has been acquired by OneRobotics, the parent company of SwitchBot. In an exclusive interview with The Verge, Nanoleaf CEO Gimmy Chu says the company will remain independent and that he and his cofounder and COO, Christian Yan, will continue to run it. "Nothing is changing operationally," says Chu, adding that there are plans for product integrations between the two smart home companies. 
The sale, which Chu characterized as "more of a merger," will provide Nanoleaf with significant resources, including a cash infusion that will, among other things, help the company grow its team at its Toronto headquarters. I …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: SwitchBot收购Nanoleaf：不仅限于照明业务
zh.summary: SwitchBot通过母公司OneRobotics收购智能照明公司Nanoleaf，计划整合产品但保持Nanoleaf独立运营。

title: Beans use an immune receptor to call in airstrikes on caterpillars
summary: Bean plants trigger an immune response that releases chemicals to attract parasitic wasps, which then attack and kill caterpillars feeding on the plant.
content: When they're being eaten, bean plants release chemicals that draw in parasitic wasps.

zh.title: 豆类植物利用免疫受体召唤寄生蜂攻击毛毛虫
zh.summary: 豆类植物触发免疫反应释放化学物质，吸引寄生蜂来攻击并杀死在植物上取食的毛毛虫。

title: Trans teens have something to say
summary: A nonbinary high school student shares their experience receiving gender-affirming medical care, highlighting impacts as major medical institutions restrict access to such services.
content:
By the time the Children's Hospital closed its doors to trans patients, Sage had already stopped taking testosterone. A nonbinary high school student, they originally received treatment for the rapid onset of puberty. The changes their body experienced felt frightening and sudden. They developed PMOS, a relatively common hormonal disorder that can lead to hair growth and irregular periods. The pandemic didn't help. Too much time to focus on scrutinizing the person in the mirror while doomscrolling. Their doctor first prescribed puberty blockers to help with their PMOS symptoms - not explicitly for trans-related reasons - and eventually recom …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: 跨性别青少年有话要说
zh.summary: 一位非二元性别的高中学生分享了获得性别肯定医疗护理的经历,强调了在主要医疗机构限制此类服务获取情况下所产生的影响。

title: How long will it take to rebuild Blue Origin's launch pad? We asked some SpaceX vets.
summary: SpaceX veterans assess the timeline and complexity required to rebuild Blue Origin's launch pad after a recent incident.
content: "Everyone is in a place where it’s no fun to be there."

zh.title: 重建蓝色起源发射台需要多长时间?我们咨询了一些SpaceX资深人士
zh.summary: SpaceX资深人士评估了近期事故后重建蓝色起源发射台所需的时间表和复杂程度。

title: The world’s largest privately owned laser just turned on
summary: Xcimer Energy, a fusion startup, powered on the world's largest privately owned laser as it pursues commercial fusion energy production.
content: Fusion startup Xcimer fired up the world's largest privately owned laser.

zh.title: 世界上最大的私有激光刚刚启动
zh.summary: 核聚变初创公司Xcimer能源启动了世界上最大的私有激光,以追求商业核聚变能源生产。

title: AI has a water problem. Google thinks it has a fix
summary: Google pledged to replenish more water than its AI data centers consume by 2030, alongside commitments to invest in local water infrastructure and identify alternative water sources.
content:
In the face of widespread backlash to the AI data center buildout throughout the US, Google is touting its efforts to minimize the environmental impact by actually increasing water for local communities. 
The company laid out five commitments around water use in a new blog post published Wednesday, including a goal to replenish more water than it uses at its data centers by 2030. Google also said it will invest in local water infrastructure, identify alternative water sources to power its facilities, and be transparent about its water use overall. 
"We're just one of dozens of players in the space," Google's global head of infrastructure a …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: AI有水资源问题。谷歌认为有解决方案
zh.summary: 谷歌承诺到2030年补充超过其AI数据中心消耗的水量，同时承诺投资当地水基础设施并寻找替代水源。

title: Google must let publishers opt out of AI Search features, rules UK
summary: The UK's Competition and Markets Authority ruled that Google must provide publishers with tools to opt out of AI Search features like AI Overviews and prevent their content from training AI models.
content:
Online publishers are getting more control over whether their websites appear in Google's AI Search features, thanks to a UK regulatory ruling. The new conduct rule imposed by the Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) requires Google to let website owners keep their content out of features like AI Overviews and prevent it from being used for the "fine-tuning" of Google's AI models. 
"In a world first, publishers will now have effective tools to prevent their content being used to power AI features in search, such as AI Overviews," the CMA announced. "This will put publishers, like news organizations, in a stronger position to negotiate co …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: 英国裁定谷歌必须允许出版商选择退出AI搜索功能
zh.summary: 英国竞争和市场管理局裁定，谷歌必须向出版商提供工具以选择退出AI搜索功能（如AI概览），并防止其内容用于训练AI模型。
